name: Eggnog Pie in Pecan Crust
recipeIngredient: Chocolate curls and Raspberries for garnish
recipeInstructions:
Pecan Crust:
Butter a 10-inch pie plate. In a medium bowl, combine pecans, sugar, and melted butter. Mix well. Press firmly into the pie plate. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes while preheating the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake the crust until lightly browned (about 15-20 minutes).
Egg Nog Filling:
In a small bowl, sprinkle gelatin over cold water and brandy, allowing it to soften. In a separate small mixing bowl, combine egg yolks and sugar in an electric mixer at high speed until the mixture forms ribbons when beaters are lifted. Pour this into a large heavy saucepan and stir in hot milk slowly, cooking over medium-low heat while stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens enough to coat the spoon (and/or thermometer registers 172°F). Do not boil. Remove from heat and stir in softened gelatin until dissolved. Stir in vanilla and rum. Pour the custard into a medium bowl set into a larger bowl of ice water and allow it to cool, stirring frequently. When it begins to set around the edges but is still slightly warm, remove from the ice bath (do not let it set completely). In the chilled bowl, beat cream to soft peaks and gradually fold into the custard. If necessary, refrigerate briefly until the filling mounds when spooned.
Pile the filling into the crust and refrigerate until set (about an hour).
Cream Topping:
In a chilled medium bowl, whip cream and confectioners sugar to stiff peaks and pile on top of the pie. Garnish with chocolate curls and raspberries.
